RULING: PETITIONER'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS DENIED; JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED IN FAVOR OF THE STATE RESPONDENTS, CALIFORNIA STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD AND CALIFORNIA REGIONAL WATER QUALITY CONTROL BOARD, SAN FRANCISCO BAY REGION AND AGAINST PETITIONERS COMMUNITIES FOR A BETTER ENVIRONMENT AND SAN FRANCISCO BAYKEEPER. THE STATE RESPONDENTS AND REAL PARTY IN INTEREST SHALL BE AWARDED COSTS. ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD IS TO BE RELEASED TO PETITIONERS. (301/JLW/)

ADDED TO LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR Motion To Reconsider &/Or Clarify The Court'S Jan.6,2003 Order Granting Petitioners' Motion For Attys' Fees & Costs
Off Calendar Mar-07-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT CALIF STATE WATER RESOURCES CONTROL BOARD Motion To Reconsider &/Or Clarify The Court'S Jan.6,2003 Order Granting Petitioners' Motion For Attys' Fees & Costs

Appearances:
Clifford Lee, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Department of Justice, Attorney General's Office, 455 Golden Gate Avenue, Suite 11000, San Francisco, CA, 94102, 415-703-5546 and Gavin G. McCabe, Esq., Deputy Attorney General, Department of Justice, Attorney General's Office, 455 Golden Gate Avenue, Suite 11000, San Francisco, CA, 94102, 415-703-5605 appearing for California State Water Resources Control Board, et al., and moving for reconsideration/clarification of attorney fees award.
Natasha Sen, Esq., BINGHAM McCUTCHEN, Three Embarcadero Center, San Francisco, CA 94114, 415, 393, 2562, appearing for real party in interest defendant Tesoro joining in the motion this day..
Adrienne Bloch, Esq., Communities for a Better Environment, 1611 Telegraph Ave., Suite 450, Oakland, CA 94612, 510-302-0430, Est. 203, and Michael R. Lozeau, Esq., Earthjustice Legal Defense Fund, 553 Salvatierra Walk, Stanford, CA 94305, 650-725-4217 appearing for plaintiff and opposing party.

No tentative ruling was issued by the Court. After argument the Court denied the Motion for Reconsideration. In terms of the Motion for Clarification the Court declared that the calculations in the proposed order were correct, that the split shall be fifty/fifty, and the multiplier shall remain at one and a half. The award is stayed pending a decision on the underlying appeal for petition on writ. The tentative ruling adopted as the Court's order on February 21, 2003, was vacated. The Attorney General shall prepare the order. (JJM/cw) Reported by Eric Throne, CSR #7855 (gps)
